criterion_selection.question: To accurately diagnose anterior ischemia, which of the following diagnostic criteria should be evaluated?
options: Presence of ST-segment depression in anterior leads
Presence of flattened T waves in inferior leads
Presence of diffuse concave ST-segment elevation in anterior leads
Presence of ST-segment depression in leads II, III, and aVF
Presence of J-point elevation in anterior leads
criterion_selection.answer: Presence of ST-segment depression in anterior leads

finding.question: Reagrding the criterion you selected, does this ECG show ST depression in at least two of the anterior leads, including leads V2, V3, V4, and V5?
options: Yes
No

decision.question: based on the finding identified above, does this patient have anterior ischemia?
options: Yes
No
Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
decision.answer: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis

criterion_selection.question: In addition to the finding you just identified, which other diagnostic criterion should be evaluated to diagnose anterior ischemia?
options: Presence of Camel-hump T waves (P on T) in anterior leads
Presence of inverted T waves in anterior leads
Presence of tall, peaked T waves in anterior leads
Presence of pathological Q waves in anterior leads
Presence of flattened T waves in anterior leads
criterion_selection.answer: Presence of inverted T waves in anterior leads

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, does this ECG show inverted T waves in at least two of the anterior leads, including leads V2, V3, V4, and V5?
options: Yes
No

decision.question: Based on all the findings identified so far, does this patient have anterior ischemia?
options: Yes
No
Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
